name: im-still-here
description: Austin Channing Brown's memoir of navigating white spaces as a Black woman — the exhaustion of code-switching, the search for Black dignity, the joy of Black community, and the declaration that 'we're still here.'
useCase: Navigating white spaces as a person of color, understanding the exhaustion of code-switching, finding Black joy and community, advocating for racial justice without being silenced.
knownRisks: This is a personal memoir and not a comprehensive guide to antiracism
Brown writes from a Christian perspective — religious readers may resonate more than secular ones
Some white readers may find the book challenging or uncomfortable, which is by design
